title: VAP Verdict
description: The server's response to a Scope Commitment, Intent Envelope, or Scope Amendment. Unlike a plain tool result, a verdict reports WHAT was decided and WHY, and is itself optionally signed so that denials are non-repudiable (mutual accountability).

verdict.description: served = request executed/accepted. clarify = need more info (maps to MCP elicitation). downgraded = accepted with reduced scope/effect. denied = refused.

result.description: Present when verdict=served and the message was an intent_call: the tool output. Shape mirrors the underlying MCP tool result.

accepted_commitment_digest.description: Present when verdict=served/downgraded for a scope_commitment or scope_amendment: digest of the commitment the server is now enforcing. The client MUST reference this state going forward.

verification.description: How the decision was reached — the audit-grade explanation.

checks.description: Named checks that ran and their outcome, e.g. 'schema_ok', 'scope_ok', 'budget_ok', 'policy:R3_ok', 'intent_consistent'.

method.description: Which verification tiers executed for this decision.

risk_score.description: The C5 risk scorer output for this call (0..1). Recorded for audit and tuning.

semantic_invoked.description: Whether the LLM judge actually fired (vs. a cached verdict or deterministic-only path).

clarification.description: Present when verdict=clarify. Maps to an MCP elicitation/create request.

schema.description: Optional JSON Schema for the structured answer expected (flat object, primitive props — matching MCP elicitation constraints).

downgrade.description: Present when verdict=downgraded: what the server reduced.

audit_ref.description: Reference to the signed audit record for this decision.

expires_at.description: Optional: when a served/cached semantic verdict ceases to be valid for reuse.

signature.description: Optional JWS over the canonicalized verdict. RECOMMENDED especially for verdict=denied/downgraded so refusals are non-repudiable and auditable by the client (mutual accountability).
